Title: Limonene enhances rice plant resistance to a piercing-sucking herbivore and rice pathogens

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct defense against brown planthopper eggs; enhances resistance to sheath blight, bacterial blight, and rice blast; volatile cue affecting herbivore host preference
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatile emission from rice plants; eggs deposited in leaf sheath are affected
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for (S)-limonene emission in this study; not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl pyrophosphate to produce the monoterpene (S)-limonene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased (S)-limonene emission, reduced BPH egg hatching, reduced lesion length caused by Rhizoctonia solani and Xanthomonas oryzae, reduced field incidence of BPH, rice blast, and sheath blight; knockout had opposite effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression in rice; qRT-PCR; GC-MS volatile profiling; BPH choice and oviposition assays; egg-hatching assays; pathogen inoculation assays; field trials
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BPH infestation strongly induced OsTPS19 expression and increased (S)-limonene emission. 2. OsTPS19 overexpression raised (S)-limonene more strongly than OsTPS20 overexpression, while knockout reduced emission. 3. Increased OsTPS19 lowered BPH egg hatching and improved resistance to Rhizoctonia solani, Xanthomonas oryzae, and field blast/sheath blight with no significant yield penalty in overexpression lines.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by brown planthopper infestation; elevated in bud during embryonic stage, sheath and leaf during seedling stage, and sheath during tillering stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in seedlings and tillering plants after infestation with 10 gravid BPH females for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Brown planthopper infestation; pathogen infection by Rhizoctonia solani and Xanthomonas oryzae; field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to engineer volatile-mediated resistance to brown planthopper and multiple rice diseases; high-expression deployment may reduce pest populations and disease without major yield penalty, but ecological tradeoff with increased striped stem borer damage should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression promoted striped stem borer damage in the field; altered volatile blend with reduced linalool and β-myrcene in healthy overexpression plants; knockout reduced panicle number and seed weight
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsTPS19 and OsTPS20 are functionally redundant for limonene production, but OsTPS19 showed a stronger effect on limonene emission and some resistance traits; competition for shared GPP precursor may reduce other monoterpenes such as linalool and β-myrcene

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct defense against brown planthopper eggs; enhances resistance to sheath blight, bacterial blight, and rice blast; volatile cue affecting herbivore host preference
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatile emission from rice plants; eggs deposited in leaf sheath are affected
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for (S)-limonene emission in this study; not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl pyrophosphate to produce the monoterpene (S)-limonene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased (S)-limonene emission, reduced BPH egg hatching, reduced lesion length caused by Rhizoctonia solani and Xanthomonas oryzae, reduced field incidence of BPH, rice blast, and sheath blight; knockout had opposite effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression in rice; qRT-PCR; GC-MS volatile profiling; BPH choice and oviposition assays; egg-hatching assays; pathogen inoculation assays; field trials
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BPH infestation induced OsTPS20 expression and increased (S)-limonene emission. 2. OsTPS20 overexpression increased, and knockout decreased, (S)-limonene release. 3. Increased OsTPS20 improved resistance to brown planthopper and major rice pathogens, though generally with weaker effects than OsTPS19 for some traits.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by brown planthopper infestation; elevated in roots during seedling stage, leaves during tillering stage, and spikes during spiking stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in seedlings and tillering plants after infestation with 10 gravid BPH females for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Brown planthopper infestation; pathogen infection by Rhizoctonia solani and Xanthomonas oryzae; field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase limonene-mediated resistance to brown planthopper and multiple diseases in rice; suitable for metabolic engineering or breeding, while monitoring ecological effects on other herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression promoted striped stem borer damage in the field; knockout reduced panicle number and seed weight
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsTPS20 is functionally redundant with OsTPS19 for limonene production and may have distinct regulatory patterns, including prior reported response to abiotic factors; overexpression had little effect on other measured volatiles compared with OsTPS19
